steps.title: Compare the two texts
steps.content: Text 1 says Nebraska structures are typically not built to resist expansive soil damage because the effects appear slowly and can be repaired. Text 2 says expansive soil damage can be mitigated by building structures on pile supports.

steps.title: Apply Text 2's solution
steps.content: A proposes measuring moisture levels, which Text 2 does not recommend. B says Nebraska engineers misjudged existing techniques, but Text 1 says they typically do not build to resist the damage. D focuses on replacing existing supports, which is not discussed. Karim's team would recommend pile supports for new structures. The answer is C.
